title: Parallel Tool Calls, TaskGroup Ownership, Ordering, and Shared Effects · Program Brief: Call Independence
summary: The agent launches every call concurrently, loses call-result order, mutates shared session state, swallows ExceptionGroup failures, and leaks tasks after cancellation. Predict authority, model and API behavior, state, tool effects, budgets, privacy, safety, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the call independence increment in structured bounded parallel tool executor. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory,…
objectives: Prove tool-call independence from inputs, effects, authority, ordering, rate limits, shared resources, and compensating behavior before parallel execution.
Propagate cancellation through provider, tool, approval, task, stream, and checkpoint boundaries and reconcile uncertain effects.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-ai-agent-python cumulative safe evaluated AI agent portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python interaction, tool, policy, state, memory,
evaluation, telemetry, and recovery models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only.
Provider APIs, Gemini models, native google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic, HTTP, credentials,
host files, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, cost, privacy review,
and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentPolicy:
    stakeholder_task_id: str
    allowed_tool_names: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_steps: int = 12
    maximum_tool_calls: int = 8
    approval_required_for_writes: bool = True


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentEvidence:
    revision: str
    model_config_digest: str
    policy_digest: str
    evaluation_digest: str
    recovery_drill_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Safe evaluated Python AI agent portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: agentpy-worked-agentpy-parallel-tool-calls-4
# Competency: agentpy-worked-agentpy-parallel-tool-calls-4.
# Case 1655: a controlled support-operations rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: telemetry and memory must remain tenant-isolated, minimized, redacted, and attributable.
# Changed case: change one eval case, rubric, model version, token price, rate limit, or latency distribution and rerun release gates.
def worked_agentpy_parallel_tool_calls_4(scheduled_calls: int = 12, completed_calls: int = 12, parallel_limit: int = 4, peak_parallel: int = 3, leaked_tasks: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "authorityworked4",
        "interaction1655",
        "tool4dekrow",
        "stateworked41655",
        "evaluation1655worked4",
        "recovery4dekrow1655",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if scheduled_calls < 0 or completed_calls < 0:
        return False, completed_calls, "parallel-count"
    if completed_calls != scheduled_calls:
        return False, completed_calls, "parallel-reconciliation"
    if parallel_limit < 1 or peak_parallel > parallel_limit:
        return False, peak_parallel, "parallel-budget"
    if leaked_tasks > 0:
        return False, leaked_tasks, "task-leak"
    assert completed_calls == scheduled_calls and leaked_tasks == 0
    return True, completed_calls, "structured-tool-concurrency"

# Evidence: agentpy-call-independence-19umty
# Competency: agentpy-call-independence.
# Case 8906: a keyboard and screen-reader interaction review.
# Operating constraint: every external effect requires explicit least-authority policy.
# Changed case: replace trusted context with untrusted indirect instructions and preserve data-versus-instruction boundaries.
def evidence_agentpy_call_independence_19umty(scheduled_calls: int = 12, completed_calls: int = 12, parallel_limit: int = 4, peak_parallel: int = 3, leaked_tasks: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:

    evidence_axes_19umty = (
        "authority19umty",
        "interaction8906",
        "toolytmu91",
        "state19umty8906",
        "evaluation890619umty",
        "recoveryytmu918906",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_19umty)) == 6
    if scheduled_calls < 0 or completed_calls < 0:
        return False, completed_calls, "parallel-count"
    if completed_calls != scheduled_calls:
        return False, completed_calls, "parallel-reconciliation"
    if parallel_limit < 1 or peak_parallel > parallel_limit:
        return False, peak_parallel, "parallel-budget"
    if leaked_tasks > 0:
        return False, leaked_tasks, "task-leak"
    assert completed_calls == scheduled_calls and leaked_tasks == 0
    return True, completed_calls, "structured-tool-concurrency"

hints: For Safe AI agents and Python Sequence Call Independence evidence · Theory Call Independence, name the stakeholder, risk, revision, Python, SDK, API, model, interaction, call, tool, authority, state, budget, approval, memory, evaluation, trace, privacy, security, accessibility, recovery, and provider-transfer boundary, Call Independence precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Safe AI agents and Python Sequence Call Independence evidence · Theory Call Independence, trace this concrete input and predict model request, interaction step, schema validation, tool choice, authorization, approval, result, state transition, memory, evaluation, trace, rejection, stop, rollback, recovery, and controlled-provider evidence before reviewing it: A theory agent engineering team handles case 4321 during a multilingual case-routing evaluation. Build deterministic Python evidence to run independent tools with bounded TaskGroup ownership, stable call-result correlation, serialized shared effects, cancellation, and cleanup; last valid checkpoint, audit trail, kill switch, and rollback path must survive failure; then replace trusted context with untrusted indirect instructions and preserve data-versus-instruction boundaries. Browser code uses pure bounded state machines and original fixed fixtures only. Real provider APIs, Gemini models, google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic native validation, HTTP, files, credentials, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, privacy review, and production effects require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Prove tool-call independence from inputs, effects, authority, ordering, rate limits, shared resources, and compensating behavior before parallel execution.

For Safe AI agents and Python Sequence Call Independence evidence · Theory Call Independence, challenge the documented misconception with changed-model, API, schema, tool, authority, result, state, loop, approval, context, memory, retry, injection, evaluation, telemetry, rate, cost, framework, sandbox, deployment, recovery, and controlled provider-transfer evidence: Multiple calls returned together by a model are necessarily independent.

steps.title: Safe AI agents and Python Defend the Call Independence model · Theory Call Independence
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Call Independence: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory agent engineering team handles case 2702 during a keyboard and screen-reader interaction review. Build deterministic Python evidence to run independent tools with bounded TaskGroup ownership, stable call-result correlation, serialized shared effects, cancellation, and cleanup; every external effect requires explicit least-authority policy; then change one eval case, rubric, model version, token price, rate limit, or latency distribution and rerun release gates. Browser code uses pure bounded state machines and original fixed fixtures only. Real provider APIs, Gemini models, google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic native validation, HTTP, files, credentials, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, privacy review, and production effects require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Prove tool-call independence from inputs, effects, authority, ordering, rate limits, shared resources, and compensating behavior before parallel execution.

Evidence target: Safe AI agents and Python Defend the Call Independence model · Theory Call Independence.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Safe AI agents and Python Defend the Call Independence model · Theory Call Independence, keep Call Independence connected to executable Python model, interaction, tool, policy, state, approval, evaluation, security, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Prove tool-call independence from inputs, effects, authority, ordering, rate limits, shared resources, and compensating behavior before parallel execution.

checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: agentpy-call-independence-19umty.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: agentpy-call-independence-19umty(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_agentpy_call_independence_19umty\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?scheduled_calls < 0 or completed_calls < 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?completed_calls != scheduled_calls)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?parallel_limit < 1 or peak_parallel > parallel_limit)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?leaked_tasks > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
